title: 10 best herbal creams for eye strain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with eye strain because of its unique blend of herbs that calm and soothe the eyes. The cream's active ingredients, such as Ginkgo biloba and chamomile, work to reduce inflammation and relax the ocular muscles, providing instant relief from fatigue and dryness. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, leaving the eyes feeling soft and refreshed. This makes it an excellent solution for individuals who spend extended periods in front of screens or experience frequent eye strain.
remedy_properties: Adenosine: Relaxes muscles and reduces tension, which helps to alleviate eye strain caused by prolonged use of digital devices or reading.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry eyes or eye fatigue.

Ginkgo biloba flavonoids: Improve blood flow and oxygenation to the eyes, reducing the risk of eye strain caused by decreased circulation, which can exacerbate conditions like computer vision syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them effective in relieving eye strain.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains antioxidants and flavonoids that protect the eyes from oxidative stress and promote healthy vision, reducing eye strain and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including ginkgo biloba extract 120mg per ounce, coconut oil 3 ounces, beeswax 2 tablespoons and vitamin e oil 100 iu.
Combine ginkgo biloba extract and coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl for melting the wax.
Add beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated into a smooth consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately two hours before whipping with an electric mixer.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes or until it becomes light and fluffy, then transfer to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with eye strain because they contain soothing and calming properties that alleviate dry, irritated eyes. The gel-like substance in aloe vera has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that penetrate deep into the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. Additionally, aloe's hydrating effects help to moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, which can become dry and tired from prolonged screen time or reading.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes caused by prolonged screen time or other eye strain causes.
Moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with eye strain.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes' delicate tissues and contribute to eye fatigue, protecting the eyes from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, aloe vera leaves help reduce inflammation and soothe dry, tired eyes.
Roots: Containing saponins and other bioactive compounds, aloe roots aid in reducing redness and itching caused by eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves weighing approximately 200-250 grams.
Mix the harvested aloe vera gel with 20 milliliters of distilled water and 10 grams of calendula flower powder.
Combine the mixture with 10 grams of cooled coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax, gently stirring until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30-40 minutes, then whip using an electric mixer.
Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to six months or in the refrigerator for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with eye strain because they contain natural ingredients that work to reduce puffiness, soothe dryness, and calm irritated eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of herbal eyebright creams, such as chamomile and calendula, help to relax the muscles around the eyes and reduce redness, providing immediate relief from fatigue and discomfort. Additionally, the moisturizing benefits of these creams help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Eyebright creams rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in eyebright herbs, like chamomile and calendula, soothe and reduce redness and swelling associated with eye strain, promoting relaxation and comfort around the eyes.

Antihistamine Properties: Eyebright creams containing antihistamines, such as quercetin, help alleviate itching and irritation caused by allergies or other environmental factors, reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant contain high levels of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhizomes: Eyebright rhizomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality euphrasia officinalis leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried euphrasia leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground euphrasia with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for 1 hour.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Whip 50 grams of beeswax with 25 milliliters of coconut oil until smooth, then blend in the cooled euphrasia-infused oil and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with eye strain because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb calends, also known as pot marigold, has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated eyes. When applied topically in a cream form, it reduces redness and swelling associated with prolonged screen time or other forms of eye strain. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue. This natural remedy provides gentle and effective relief from eye strain without harsh chemicals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye strain, making it an effective remedy for conjunctivitis and other eye irritations.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream can neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, thereby reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration and promoting overall eye health.

Soothing and lubricating properties: Pot marigold cream's moisturizing and soothing effects can help calm irritated or dry eyes, reducing itchiness, burning, and stinging sensations caused by prolonged screen time or other factors that contribute to eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Barks: Containing compounds like salicylic acid and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and irritation caused by eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers and clean them thoroughly by gently brushing off any dirt or debris.
Combine one part calendula flowers with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the flowers to infuse their medicinal properties into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter squeezing out any remaining liquid and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a creamy texture suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with eye strain because they contain soothing botanicals that calm the eyes and relax the surrounding muscles. The herbs, such as chamomile and calendula, work to reduce inflammation and quiet the constant blinking that can occur when our eyes are tired or stressed. As we apply the cream around the orbital bone, it cools and calms the area, providing instant relief from dryness and irritation. This natural remedy allows us to rest our eyes, reducing fatigue and promoting clear vision for hours to come.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds (e.g. flavonoids, anthocyanins): These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, relieving discomfort and pain associated with eye strain.
Antioxidants (e.g. vitamin C, E): Antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to eye strain, promoting healthy vision and reducing oxidative stress.
Cicatrizant properties (e.g. aloe vera): Cicatrizant agents help promote tissue repair and reduce scar formation in the eyes, which can be beneficial for eye strains caused by dryness or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce puffiness and soothe tired eyes.
Barks: Containing astringent properties, the barks can help to tighten loose skin around the eyes and reduce dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and rinse them gently with cold water to remove impurities.
Dry the camellia sinensis leaves in a warm oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine one tablespoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves with two tablespoons of coconut oil and five drops of vitamin e oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture overnight at room temperature allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the oils for eight hours.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing the cream in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with eye strain because they contain calming and soothing properties that alleviate the discomfort caused by prolonged screen time. The herbal extract of lavender has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can ease dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, providing long-lasting relief from eye strain and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms of eye strain.
Calming and sedative properties: Lavender's calming effects help soothe the eyes and mind, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can exacerbate eye strain and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective in alleviating eye strain.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress and promote healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and add them to a large glass jar.
Pour in 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil and stir until the mixture is well combined, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Place the jar in a warm water bath at 60°c for 30 minutes to steep the lavender flowers in the coconut oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids, and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
Mix one part of the cooled infused oil with one part of beeswax and stir until melted, then pour the cream into glass jars and label for use within 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with eye strain because of its soothing and calming properties. The herbal extract has anti-inflammatory agents that reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing instant relief from tired and irritated eyes. Additionally, chamomile's natural oils help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing fatigue and tension. By applying chamomile cream directly to the eyes or under the orbital bone, it provides a gentle and effective way to combat eye strain caused by prolonged screen use, reading, or other activities that require visual attention.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye strain by soothing and calming the eyes.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
Sedative: Chamomile's sedative properties help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, which can alleviate eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps calm and soothe irritated eyes, reducing eye strain.
Roots: Containing sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve pressure and tension around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried chamomile flowers measuring 2 teaspoons.
Combine the dried herbs with carrier oils like coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the herbal infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids after 10-15 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and beeswax to the strained herbal liquid and stir until melted and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with eye strain because they provide a calming and refreshing sensation to the eyes. The menthol in peppermint oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling caused by prolonged screen use or reading. Additionally, the cooling effect of peppermint can increase blood flow to the eyes, reducing fatigue and tension. As a result, herbal peppermint creams can be an effective way to alleviate eye strain and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, methanol, and limonene, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve redness in the eyes caused by prolonged screen time.
Cooing and calming effects: The cooling sensation of peppermint creams helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing feelings of dryness and fatigue associated with eye strain.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint creams contain antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, further reducing the risk of eye strain and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, peppermint barks provide a natural remedy to reduce eye fatigue and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20 grams of high-quality peppermint oil mentha x piperita and 100 grams of beeswax pellets in a double boiler setup.
Melt the beeswax pellets in the double boiler for exactly 10 minutes, ensuring complete dissolution without overheating.
Add the weighed mentha x piperita essential oil to the melted beeswax mixture and stir gently until well combined.
Continue heating the mixture at a low temperature for an additional 5 minutes to facilitate even penetration of ingredients.
Remove the cream base from heat, let it cool down to room temperature, and pour into sterilized containers for storage or immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with eye strain because they contain a unique blend of antioxidants and soothing agents that calm and relax the delicate skin around the eyes. The herbal extracts in these creams work to reduce puffiness, dark circles, and dryness, while also providing a cooling sensation that relieves tension and fatigue. This results in improved vision clarity and reduced eye strain, making it an effective natural solution for individuals who spend extended periods in front of digital screens or engage in prolonged visual tasks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by blue light and UV radiation, reducing eye fatigue and strain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyelids and conjunctiva, alleviating dryness, itchiness, and redness associated with eye strain.

Wound-healing and tissue repair: Asiatic Pennywort's wound-healing properties promote the regeneration of damaged corneal tissues and epithelial cells, helping to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reduce discomfort, and accelerate recovery from eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort caused by eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 200g of high-quality centella asiatica extract powder into a clean glass bowl.
Combine 100g of sweet almond oil and 50g of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add the centella asiatica extract powder to the saucepan mixture and stir until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours to allow it to thicken fully.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Echinacea contains compounds like cichoric acid and echinacein that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye strain.
Antioxidant: Echinacea has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, a common contributor to eye fatigue and strain.

Immune system support: Echinacea is believed to have immunomodulatory effects, which may help boost the body's natural defenses against infections that can cause eye problems, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them a valuable ingredient in eye strain creams.
Buds: The buds of the Kansas coneflower are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes, helping to alleviate eye strain symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Combine the herbs with 40 grams of coconut oil in a clean glass jar for infusing.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding solids and filtering again.
Mix the filtered oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter then heat at 100°f for 30 minutes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with eye strain because they contain the herb Echinacea, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's moisturizing ingredients also help to reduce dryness and fatigue, while its antioxidant-rich formula shields the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors. As a result, Kansas coneflower creams provide long-lasting relief from eye strain caused by prolonged screen time, reading, or other visual activities.

Blueberry creams helps with eye strain because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. The herbal ingredients work to soothe and calm the eyes, relieving tension and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time or other visual demands. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing irritation, blueberry creams provide natural relief for dry, irritated eyes and improve overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
Quercetin: As a potent anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin helps alleviate redness and irritation associated with eye strain, promoting comfortable vision and reduced dryness.

Vitamin C: This essential nutrient boosts collagen production and strengthens the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes, helping to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles caused by prolonged screen use.
remedy_parts: Roots: Blueberry roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them a great addition to creams for relieving eye strain.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of vaccinium myrtillus leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered leaf with 30 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60°c while stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts completely then remove from heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized containers and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with eye strain because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and natural oils that work together to soothe and calm the eyes. The herb fennel has been traditionally used to treat various eye disorders, including conjunctivitis and dry eyes, by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tear production. When applied topically as a cream, fennel's active ingredients penetrate deep into the skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from fatigue, dryness, and discomfort associated with prolonged screen time or other visual strain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel creams can reduce swelling and irritation caused by prolonged screen time, allergies, or dry eyes, providing quick relief from redness and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic agents help relax the eye muscles and soothe dry, itchy, or burning sensations, easing the strain on the eyes and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing strain and discomfort.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to treat eye problems due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve visual clarity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves in their peak potency.
Dry foeniculum vulgare leaves using low-temperature air circulation method.
Combine dried foeniculum vulgare with carrier oil in a 10:1 ratio.
Infuse the herb mixture under gentle heat for 2 hours and 15 minutes.
Filter and bottle the cream, storing it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with eye strain because of its soothing and calming properties. The natural oils present in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, work together to relax the eyes and reduce fatigue. When applied topically to the skin around the eyes, these oils can help to ease tension and alleviate symptoms of digital eye strain caused by prolonged screen use or lack of sleep. This results in improved visual comfort and reduced redness, allowing for a more restful and rejuvenated gaze.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass cream, such as citral and geraniol, help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with eye strain.

Relaxing and calming properties: The essential oils present in lemongrass cream, particularly citronella and neroli, have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce tension and stress that can contribute to eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemongrass roots help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to eye strain.
Rhyzomes: With its anti-inflammatory properties, lemongrass rhyzomes soothe irritated eyes and relieve discomfort caused by prolonged screen time or reading.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered cymbopogon citratus leaves with 200ml of sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 60°c before adding 10ml of vitamin e oil and 5ml of aloe vera gel.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it set at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams helps with eye strain because they provide a soothing and nourishing treatment for dry, irritated eyes. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of roselle help to reduce redness and puffiness, while its moisturizing agents hydrate the eyes and surrounding skin. Additionally, roselle's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by blue light exposure and other environmental stressors. As a result, using roselle creams can provide fast and effective relief from eye strain and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Roselle cream's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in roselle cream may help to reduce puffiness, redness, and irritation associated with eye strain, promoting a more comfortable and relaxed ocular environment.

Humectant properties: Roselle cream's ability to retain moisture helps to soothe dry, irritated eyes and provide long-lasting hydration, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals who spend extended periods in front of digital screens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contain soothing and moisturizing properties that reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powder with 100ml of distilled water to create an infusion, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 50g of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20g of beeswax pellets in a double boiler, heating until melted and fully incorporated.
Remove from heat, stir in 2% vitamin e oil and let cool to a thick consistency before pouring into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with eye strain because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to soothe and calm the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of sweet briar help reduce redness and irritation, while the chamomile and lavender relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and fatigue. As a result, herbal sweet briar creams can provide fast and effective relief from the discomfort and visual distortion associated with eye strain, allowing you to focus clearly and comfortably for hours on end.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory activity: Sweet briar cream's vasodilatory property helps to dilate blood vessels in the eyes, increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation, which can alleviate eye strain caused by prolonged computer use or other visual demands.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in sweet briar cream help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue, dryness, and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Sweet briar cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with eye strain, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated or tired eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Sweet Briar are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Sweet Briar are used as they contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve vision clarity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa rubiginosa flowers and dry them properly in a well-ventilated area for at least 72 hours.
Combine 30 grams of dried rosa rubiginosa flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of the rosa rubiginosa infused oil together in a double boiler until smooth and creamy, allowing it to cool for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with eye strain because of its calming and soothing properties. The natural oils present in thyme, such as thymol and borneol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles surrounding the eyes. When applied topically, these compounds can help alleviate the discomfort and fatigue associated with extended screen time or dry environments. As a result, thyme creams provide relief from eye strain, reducing redness, itchiness, and irritation while promoting healthy, comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by prolonged screen time or other forms of eye strain.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant properties combat free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to eye strain, promoting healthy vision and reducing oxidative stress.

Anti-allergic properties: Thyme cream's anti-allergic compounds help soothe itchy, irritated eyes caused by allergies or sensitivity to airborne particles, providing relief from eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the thyme plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their ability to calm and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: Thyme barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate redness associated with eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a trusted source and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground thymus vulgaris with 20 grams of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water and stir occasionally until the wax melts fully after 10-15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 40°c before whipping with an electric mixer for approximately 5-7 minutes until thickened.
Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with eye strain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb sage has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of digital eye fatigue such as dryness, redness, and itchiness. By applying a sage cream to the affected area, individuals can quickly and effectively ease discomfort and improve overall visual comfort. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals who spend extended periods in front of screens or engage in activities that require intense visual focus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage cream's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain like redness and itching.
Calming and soothing properties: Herbal sage contains calming compounds like apigenin, which can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and discomfort caused by eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye strain, making them an effective ingredient in eye care creams.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain antioxidants that protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by blue light exposure, which can contribute to eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried salvia officinalis leaves in a clean glass container for infusion.
Combine 10g of dried leaves with 100ml of boiling distilled water and let steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax pellets with 40g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the strained infusion with the melted wax-oil mixture and stir until thickened to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with eye strain because of its soothing and calming properties, which can effectively reduce the irritation and fatigue caused by prolonged screen time or other visual activities. The cream's ability to relax the muscles around the eyes and calm the nervous system helps to alleviate symptoms such as dryness, itchiness, and redness associated with eye strain. Additionally, lemon balm's natural anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, promoting a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye strain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye strain.
Anxiolytic (stress-reducing) properties: Lemon balm's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related eye strain by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to make soothing topical preparations that calm irritated eyes and reduce puffiness.
Buds: The tender buds of the lemon balm plant contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to protect and soothe the delicate tissues around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves weighing 10 grams.
Combine harvested leaves with 100 milliliters of high-proof vegetable oil and infuse for four hours.
Filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the filtered liquid and heat in a double boiler until melted.
Remove from heat stir in 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil and pour into sterilized containers.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye strain are a natural solution to alleviate the discomfort caused by prolonged screen time, reading, or other visual activities. These creams contain ingredients such as chamomile, calendula, and bilberry extract that help soothe and calm irritated eyes. Examples of herbal creams include products containing eyebright, ginkgo biloba, and green tea extracts that reduce inflammation and improve tear production. By using these creams, individuals can experience relief from eye strain, improving their overall visual comfort and allowing them to focus on tasks without distraction or fatigue.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye strain with herbal creams is to apply a topical cream containing soothing herbs such as chamomile, calendula, or eyebright. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Massage the cream gently onto the affected area, focusing on the orbital bone surrounding the eye. Repeat as needed throughout the day to alleviate discomfort and promote relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye strain that are treated with herbal creams are headaches, facial tension, and dry skin. These issues can be alleviated by using topical creams containing herbs like chamomile, calendula, and aloe vera, which soothe and calm irritated skin. Herbal creams may also contain anti-inflammatory properties, such as arnica and stinging nettle, to reduce swelling and pain associated with these conditions.
